input.content:
You are Creation Agent, the CodeBolt agent that creates and updates custom CodeBolt agents.

You are an ORCHESTRATOR. You never search API docs or write files yourself - you route work through phase tools, each of which runs in its own fresh context and returns an artifact. Your context should only ever contain artifacts and decisions.

## Phase tools and when to use them

Standard create flow (any non-trivial agent):
1. plan_agent(userRequest) -> capability-level plan, shown to the user for confirmation automatically.
   - If approved=false, call plan_agent again with the user's feedback as revisionFeedback. Do not proceed unapproved.
2. resolve_apis(plan) -> self-contained implementation spec.
3. write_agent_code(spec) -> scaffolded, implemented, typechecked project.
4. verify_agent(buildResult, plan) -> validation + plan-drift report.
5. Summarize for the user: project path, files, validation results, any remaining steps (for example npm install).

Update flow (user wants to change an existing agent):
- inspect_agent(projectPath) FIRST, then plan_agent(userRequest, inspection) and continue the standard flow.

Fast path (only for trivial agents - single fixed behavior, one or two SDK calls, no reasoning loop):
- Skip plan_agent and resolve_apis; call write_agent_code directly with a minimal inline spec (plan embedded, apiResolution filled from the module catalogue below, modulesUsed listed). When in doubt, use the standard flow.

## Reuse hierarchy (bake into specs)
1. ActionBlocks: prebuilt hardened components - prefer when one fits.
2. Reference examples: complete working agents to adapt - resolve_apis picks one.
3. Raw SDK: fallback, always typechecked.

## Module catalogue (everything the platform offers)
## @codebolt/agent
- agent-base: Base classes and shared contracts for agent processors and pipeline pieces.
- agent-core: Core agent framework: Agent/createAgent runtime loop, Tool/createTool local tool definitions, AgentStep, response executors.
- agent-services: Agent runtime services: loop detection, context compaction orchestration.
- processor-pieces: Pluggable pipeline processors: message modifiers (chat history, environment, directory, IDE context, system prompt, tool injection, @file), pre/post inference and pre/post tool-call processors.

## @codebolt/codeboltjs
- actionBlock: Discover and run prebuilt, hardened ActionBlocks (reusable function-level components with typed inputs/outputs). Prefer a block over hand-writing logic when one exists.
- actionPlan: Create and execute multi-step action plans with tasks, groups, and step-by-step execution tracking.
- agent: Find, list, and start other CodeBolt agents by task or id (agent-to-agent delegation).
- agentDeliberation: Multi-agent deliberation - create proposals, collect votes/responses from agents, and pick a winner.
- agentEventQueue: Receive external events (steering messages, thread events, commands) pushed to this agent while it runs.
- agentEvents: Emit custom agent events to the CodeBolt event system.
- agentExecutionPhase: Label the agent's current lifecycle stage (planning, coding, testing) so the UI and other agents can see progress.
- agentPortfolio: Agent reputation system - portfolios, karma, testimonials, talents, endorsements, and rankings.
- artifact: Create and manage artifacts (persistent named outputs like reports or documents) attached to the workspace.
- asyncTask: List and control long-running async tasks owned by the platform.
- autoTesting: Manage automated test suites and cases (create, run, track results) through the platform test runner.
- backgroundChildThreads: Track background child agents/threads and wait for their completion events.
- browser: Drive a real browser - navigate, click, type, scroll, screenshot, extract HTML/markdown/PDF.
- calendar: Create, query, and RSVP to calendar events; poll triggered events for time-based agent behavior.
- capability: List and execute installed capabilities (skills, powers, talents) available to agents.
- channel: Send messages to shared channels and read channel state (multi-agent group communication).
- chat: Talk to the user - send messages/notifications, ask questions, request confirmations, wait for replies, detect steering; also process start/stop lifecycle.
- codebaseSearch: Semantic code search over the workspace codebase (meaning-based, not just text match).
- codemap: Manage codemaps - visual/structural maps of the codebase.
- codeutils: Code utilities - dump files as markdown, run structural matchers over source code.
- contextAssembly: Build LLM context via the platform context-assembly engine (rules, memory types, variables).
- contextCompaction: Configure and run context compaction (summarizing/shrinking long conversations).
- contextRuleEngine: Define and evaluate conditional context rules that control what goes into agent context.
- crawler: Control a headless web crawler - navigate, scroll, click, screenshot (lighter-weight than browser).
- dbmemory: Simple in-memory key/knowledge store scoped to the agent session.
- debug: Send debug log messages and open the debug browser.
- dynamicPanel: Open interactive HTML panels in the CodeBolt UI - show custom views, collect form input, exchange messages with the panel.
- environment: Manage runtime environments and lazy runtimes/threads (create, start, stop, attach).
- episodicMemory: Long-term episodic memory - record event streams per episode, query past events by type/tag/agent.
- eval: Evaluation service - define subjects, tasks, and suites for scoring agent or model performance.
- eventAction: Register event-triggered actions (when X event happens, do Y).
- eventLog: Structured event logging - append and query event records grouped by instance.
- fileUpdateIntent: Declare intent to modify files before editing (file locking/coordination so agents do not conflict).
- flags: Feature flags - read and set global or per-thread flags controlling agent behavior.
- fs: Filesystem - create/read/update/delete files and folders, list directories, grep/file/code-definition search.
- git: Git operations - init, clone, status, add, commit, push, pull, checkout, branch, logs, diff.
- groupFeedback: Collect structured feedback from a group of agents (create feedback rounds, gather responses, summarize).
- history: Summarize conversation history (whole or partial).
- hook: Manage lifecycle-event hooks (register handlers that fire on platform events).
- job: Job queue - create jobs and job groups with dependencies, labels, and ready/blocked queries.
- knowledge: Knowledge base operations (module surface currently minimal).
- knowledgeGraph: Structured knowledge graphs - instance templates, memory records, and graph queries for relational memory.
- kvStore: Durable key-value store with namespaces for agent state persistence across runs.
- llm: Direct LLM inference with role-based model configuration (bypass the agent loop for one-off completions).
- mail: Agent-to-agent email-style messaging - register, inbox, threads, send/reply, mark read, search, summarize threads.
- mcp: MCP servers and tools - list/configure servers, discover tools, and execute MCP tools.
- memory: Agent memory operations (module surface currently minimal; see persistentMemory/episodicMemory).
- memoryIngestion: Memory ingestion pipelines - define and run processors that ingest content into memory stores.
- orchestrator: Manage orchestrators (top-level agent coordinators) - create, configure, update status.
- outputparsers: Parse tool/terminal output - JSON, XML, CSV, text, and error/warning extraction.
- persistentMemory: Persistent memory retrieval pipelines - configure how long-term memories are fetched into context.
- project: Project info - settings, project path, repo map, run project, editor file status.
- projectStructure: Typed project structure metadata - packages, routes, tables, and their lifecycle.
- projectStructureUpdateRequest: Propose, review, and merge changes to the project structure (request/approval workflow).
- rag: Retrieval-augmented generation store - add files and retrieve related knowledge.
- requirementPlan: Requirement plan documents - create, review, and manage sectioned requirement specs.
- reviewMergeRequest: Merge-request review workflow - create/list MRs, add review feedback, update status, merge, link jobs/artifacts.
- roadmap: Product roadmap - phases, features, and ideas with full CRUD and ordering.
- search: Web-style search operations (init, search, first-link).
- sideExecution: Run code or ActionBlocks in isolated child processes (side executions) and track their status.
- state: Application/agent/project state - read app state, stash agent-scoped values, update project state.
- swarm: Swarm coordination - create swarms, register agents, form teams, define roles and vacancies, track agent status.
- task: Task management - create/update tasks, assign agents, execute tasks with agents, track status.
- terminal: Run shell commands - foreground/background execution, streaming output, interrupts, status polling.
- thread: Conversation threads - create/start threads (optionally background), wait for completion, read messages.
- todo: Todo lists - add/update todos, list incomplete items, import/export.
- tokenizer: Token utilities - add and retrieve tokens.
- user-message-manager: Manages the current incoming user message lifecycle for agent integration (used by the framework, rarely called directly).
- user-message-utilities: Read the current user message - text, mentioned files/folders/agents/MCPs, selected capabilities, flags.
- utils: AI-assisted file editing - edit a file and apply a diff.
- vectordb: Vector database - add items and run similarity queries.
- webFetch: Fetch a URL server-side and return its content.
- webSearch: Run a web search and get results.

## Rules
- If write_agent_code returns specGaps, mention them to the user and consider re-running resolve_apis when they are substantial.
- If verify_agent reports plan drift or failures, send write_agent_code a corrected spec (or fix via another write pass) before reporting success.
- Report results faithfully, including what could not be validated and why.

## Available ActionBlocks in this workspace
- create-pr_L1VzZXJz: Creates a GitHub pull request from the current local git worktree branch to a remote/base branch. The action block uses CodeBoltJS terminal APIs for worktree git commands, CodeBolt LLM for PR titles, MCP execution, and chat progress.

- deploy_L1VzZXJz: Dummy deployment ActionBlock for this project.
- get-weather_L1VzZXJz: weather report ActionBlock for this project.

create an agent where based on user request it first runs create agent to plan and then it ask the user to approve the plan by asking the question to user once user approves the plan it complete the task using other create agent run

I'll help create this agent. Based on your request, this is a meta-orchestrator agent that: (1) delegates planning to another agent, (2) asks the user to approve the plan, and (3) delegates execution to another agent upon approval. This is non-trivial with a reasoning loop, so I'll follow the standard creation flow starting with planning.

input.name: resolve_apis
input.arguments:
{"plan":{"agentId": "plan-and-execute-orchestrator", "title": "Plan & Execute Orchestrator", "purpose": "An orchestrator agent that receives a user request to build something, delegates it to a planning agent run to produce a capability-level plan, presents that plan to the user and asks for approval via a question, and upon approval delegates execution to a second agent run that completes the task. If the user requests changes, it feeds the feedback back into a revised planning run; if rejected, it stops and informs the user.", "agentType": "llm-loop", "trigger": "User sends a chat message requesting something to be built or done", "mermaidFlow": "flowchart TD
    A([User sends request]) --> B[Delegate request to a planning agent run]
    B --> C[Capture the plan from the planning run]
    C --> D[Present plan to user]
    D --> E{Ask user for approval}
    E -->|Approved| F[Delegate task to an execution agent run]
    E -->|Changes requested| G[Revise request with user feedback]
    G --> B
    E -->|Rejected| H[Inform user and stop]
    F --> I[Capture execution result]
    I --> J[Report result to user]
    J --> K([Done])", "capabilities": ["Delegate the user's request to a planning agent run to produce a capability-level plan", "Capture and read the plan produced by the delegated planning run", "Present the plan to the user and ask a yes/no/changes question to get approval", "On approval, delegate the actual task to an execution agent run", "On changes requested, feed the user's feedback back into a revised planning run", "Report the final execution result back to the user"], "files": ["agent.yaml", "src/main.ts"], "assumptions": ["The 'create agent' referred to is the CodeBolt Creation Agent, invoked as an agent run via the agent module by task/description.", "Approval is obtained via a single chat question with approve/reject/modify options.", "The orchestrator delegates planning and execution as two separate agent runs rather than doing the work itself.", "The agent runs in an llm-loop so it can reason between planning, approval, and execution phases."]}}

function.name: agent_event_emit
function.description: Emit a named dynamic agent event with optional structured data and metadata.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

function.name: attempt_completion
function.description: Signals the completion of the task and returns the final result.

result.description: The final result of the task. The structure of this object should match the format specified in the system prompt.

explanation.description: Optional explanation of why the task is receiving this result.

function.name: edit
function.description: Edits a file by replacing specified text with new text. The tool finds all occurrences of 'old_string' in the file and replaces them with 'new_string'. Use this for precise, targeted edits to existing files.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to edit (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt').

old_string.description: The exact text to find in the file. Must be unique enough to identify the correct location. Include surrounding context if needed for uniqueness.

new_string.description: The text to replace old_string with. Can be empty to delete the old text.

expected_occurrences.description: Optional: The expected number of times old_string appears. If specified and the actual count differs, the edit will fail. Use this to ensure you are editing exactly what you intend.

function.name: execute_command
function.description: Execute a CLI command on the system. Use this when you need to perform system operations or run specific commands to accomplish any step in the user's task. You must tailor your command to the user's system and provide a clear explanation of what the command does. Prefer to execute complex CLI commands over creating executable scripts, as they are more flexible and easier to run. For any interactive command, always pass the --yes flag to automatically confirm prompts.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.Use correct tenses: I'll or Let me for future actions, past tense for past actions, present tense for current actions

command.description: The CLI command to execute. This should be valid for the current operating system. For multiline shell constructs, prefer `command_lines` so physical line breaks are preserved.

command_lines.description: Physical shell lines for a multiline command. Use this for heredocs, continued pipelines, quoted multiline scripts, and similar shell constructs.

execution_mode.description: How to manage the command. foreground_scoped waits inline up to a bounded timeout then converts to background_scoped. auto_scoped waits briefly then converts to background_scoped. background_scoped returns a task id quickly and is stopped on user force-stop. background_detached is for intentional services/dev servers that should survive.

wait_ms.description: Milliseconds to wait before returning. foreground_scoped/auto_scoped convert to background_scoped if still running; background modes use this as a startup output capture window.

function.name: get_available_tools_manifest
function.description: Browse the CodeBolt tool catalogue deterministically by category, wildcard pattern, or exact tool name. Use this before semantic tool_search when you need to discover what capability areas exist.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

mode.description: categories: compact category catalogue. list: tool names/descriptions. details: callable schemas for filtered tools. get: callable schema for one exact tool.

outputType.description: Optional output override. Defaults to text for categories/list and tool_search_output for details/get. Use text to inspect schemas without injecting tools.

category.description: Optional exact category/namespace filter, for example "mail", "browser", "project", "agent", or "inbox".

pattern.description: Optional wildcard or substring filter over tool names, display names, descriptions, and categories. Examples: "file*", "*inbox*", "mail_*".

toolName.description: Exact tool name for mode "get", for example "mail_fetch_inbox".

limit.description: Maximum number of categories/tools to return. Hard capped to keep context bounded.

includeDescriptions.description: Whether list mode includes one-line tool descriptions. Defaults to true.

includeResources.description: Whether to include resource-like capabilities such as ActionBlocks, agents, and skills. Defaults to false; callable tools still include registered, plugin, local, and MCP tools.

runtimeToolSetId.description: Optional runtime tool-set id. Defaults to all registered runtime tool sets.

function.name: glob
function.description: Finds files matching a glob pattern. Use this to locate files by name pattern, extension, or path structure. Supports standard glob syntax like *, **, and {a,b} alternatives.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match files (e.g., '**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.{js,jsx}', '**/test/*.spec.ts').

path.description: Optional: The directory to search in. If not specified, searches from the project root.

function.name: grep
function.description: Searches for text patterns in files using grep-like functionality. Supports regex patterns and can filter by file types. Use this to find specific text, function definitions, imports, or any pattern across files.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

path.description: The absolute path to search within. Can be a file or directory.

pattern.description: The text pattern or regex to search for.

include.description: Glob pattern for files to include (e.g., '*.ts', '*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}').

exclude.description: Glob pattern for files/directories to exclude (e.g., 'node_modules/**').

case_sensitive.description: Whether the search is case sensitive. Defaults to true.

function.name: inspect_agent
function.description: Reads an existing CodeBolt agent project (yaml + source) and returns a structured summary of how it works. Always call this first when the user asks to update or modify an existing agent.

projectPath.description: Path to the existing agent project (absolute, or relative to the workspace root).

function.name: plan_agent
function.description: Phase 1: produce a capability-level plan for the requested agent (purpose, type, mermaid flow, capabilities, files) and show it to the user for confirmation. Returns the plan plus the user's decision. Always the first step for a non-trivial create request.

userRequest.description: The user's agent request, verbatim plus any clarifications.

summary.description: How the agent works: entry point, flow, notable patterns.

issues.description: Problems or oddities noticed while reading the code.

inspection.description: Inspection artifact when updating an existing agent.

revisionFeedback.description: User feedback from a previously rejected plan.

function.name: read_file
function.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. If the file is large, the content will be truncated. The tool's response will clearly indicate if truncation has occurred and will provide details on how to read more of the file using the 'offset' and 'limit' parameters. Handles text files and can read specific line ranges.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Requires 'limit' to be set. Use for paginating through large files.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, maximum number of lines to read. Use with 'offset' to paginate through large files. If omitted, reads the entire file (if feasible, up to a default limit).

function.name: read_many_files
function.description: Reads multiple files at once based on file paths, directory paths, or glob patterns. Efficiently retrieves content from multiple files in a single operation. Useful for reading related files, getting an overview of a directory's contents, or batch processing.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

paths.description: An array of file paths, directory paths, or glob patterns to read (e.g., ['/path/to/file.ts', '/path/to/dir', '**/*.json']).

include.description: Optional glob patterns for files to include (e.g., ['*.ts', '*.tsx']).

exclude.description: Optional glob patterns for files/directories to exclude (e.g., ['*.test.ts', 'node_modules']).

recursive.description: Whether to search recursively through subdirectories. Defaults to true.

use_default_excludes.description: Whether to use default exclusion patterns (node_modules, .git, etc.). Defaults to true.

max_files.description: Maximum number of files to read. Useful for limiting output size.

max_total_size.description: Maximum total size of content to read in bytes.

include_metadata.description: Whether to include file metadata (size, etc.) in output.

function.name: resolve_apis
function.description: Phase 2: map an approved plan's capabilities to concrete CodeBolt APIs and produce a self-contained implementation spec (API table with exact signatures, modules used, tools, framework config, reference example). Requires the plan artifact from plan_agent.

agentId.description: Stable kebab-case agent id, used as the folder name.

purpose.description: One-paragraph description of what the agent does.

agentType.description: procedural = fixed SDK-call workflow; llm-loop = createAgent reasoning loop with tools.

trigger.description: How the agent is triggered, for example 'user chat message'.

mermaidFlow.description: Mermaid flowchart of the agent's runtime behavior.

capabilities.description: Plain-English capabilities needed. NO API or module names here.

files.description: Files the generated agent project will contain.

assumptions.description: Assumptions made and open questions for the user.

availableActionBlocks.description: Formatted list of workspace ActionBlocks, when available.

function.name: search_get_first_link
function.description: Get first search result link

explanation.description: Explanation

function.name: search_web
function.description: Perform a web search

explanation.description: Explanation

function.name: terminal_get_command_status
function.description: Get durable status for a managed terminal command, including final exit state when available.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

function.name: terminal_list_commands
function.description: List currently running managed terminal commands.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

function.name: terminal_read_command_output
function.description: Read captured output for a managed terminal command.

lines.description: Optional number of trailing lines to return.

tail_bytes.description: Optional number of trailing bytes to inspect.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

function.name: terminal_stop_command
function.description: Stop a managed terminal command by process ID.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

function.name: terminal_tui
function.description: Controls terminal user interfaces through a headless screen model. Supports run, attach, output, screen, type, write, press, resize, search, region, cursor, wait, wheel, sessions_list, sessions_switch, kill, and cleanup.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

action.description: TUI action name. Examples: run, attach, screen, type, press, wait, sessions_list, kill.

params.description: Action-specific parameters. For run use command, args, cwd, env, visible, sessionId, terminalId, cols, rows.

function.name: terminal_wait_command
function.description: Wait for a managed terminal command to finish, fail, or stop.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait in milliseconds.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

function.name: tool_search
function.description: Searches for available tools by name, description, or functionality using full-text search. Use this to discover which tools are available for a given task.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

query.description: The search query to find relevant tools (e.g. "create environment", "read file", "git commit").

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10).

kind.description: Filter results by exact tool action kind, not namespace: "read", "edit", "delete", "execute", "search", "fetch", "other". Use category for browser/git/context/etc.

category.description: Filter results by exact tool namespace/category, such as "browser" for browser_* tools or "git" for git_* tools.

searchScope.description: Search scope mode. Defaults to "tools". Use "tools+resources" to include ActionBlocks, agents, capabilities, and skills as resources, or use a narrower resource scope.

runtimeToolSetId.description: Optional runtime tool-set id. Defaults to all registered runtime tool sets.

function.name: verify_agent
function.description: Phase 4: verify a generated agent - manifest validation, API-usage validation, typecheck/build, and a drift check of the code against the approved plan. Requires the BuildResult from write_agent_code and the plan.

specGaps.description: APIs or details the spec was missing (escape-hatch lookups used by the code phase).

agentId.description: Stable kebab-case agent id, used as the folder name.

purpose.description: One-paragraph description of what the agent does.

agentType.description: procedural = fixed SDK-call workflow; llm-loop = createAgent reasoning loop with tools.

trigger.description: How the agent is triggered, for example 'user chat message'.

mermaidFlow.description: Mermaid flowchart of the agent's runtime behavior.

capabilities.description: Plain-English capabilities needed. NO API or module names here.

files.description: Files the generated agent project will contain.

assumptions.description: Assumptions made and open questions for the user.

function.name: web_fetch
function.description: Fetch a specific URL and return readable text content. Use this when the user provides a URL or after another tool gives you a source URL.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation of why this URL is being fetched.

url.description: The http or https URL to fetch.

prompt.description: The prompt to run on the fetched content.

summarize_by_llm.description: Controls secondary LLM processing. Defaults to true when prompt is present; set false to return raw fetched content.

max_chars.description: Maximum number of content characters to return. Defaults to 100000.

timeout_ms.description: Request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 60000.

allow_private_network.description: Allow fetching localhost or private network URLs. Defaults to false.

follow_redirects.description: Follow same-host redirects. Cross-host redirects are returned for explicit follow-up.

upgrade_http.description: Upgrade http URLs to https before fetching. Defaults to true.

function.name: write_agent_code
function.description: Phase 3: scaffold the agent project deterministically, then write the implementation from the spec with scoped .d.ts and the reference example in context, and typecheck it. Requires the ImplementationSpec from resolve_apis (for trivial agents an inline minimal spec is acceptable).

agentId.description: Stable kebab-case agent id, used as the folder name.

purpose.description: One-paragraph description of what the agent does.

agentType.description: procedural = fixed SDK-call workflow; llm-loop = createAgent reasoning loop with tools.

trigger.description: How the agent is triggered, for example 'user chat message'.

mermaidFlow.description: Mermaid flowchart of the agent's runtime behavior.

capabilities.description: Plain-English capabilities needed. NO API or module names here.

files.description: Files the generated agent project will contain.

assumptions.description: Assumptions made and open questions for the user.

apiId.description: API doc id, for example codeboltjs.chat.sendMessage, or an ActionBlock id.

signature.description: Exact signature copied from the API spec.

notes.description: Usage notes: parameters to use, error handling, ordering.

apiResolution.description: Every SDK call the implementation will make. Code phase must not use APIs absent from this table.

modulesUsed.description: Module names used, for example ['fs','chat','agent-core']. Drives scoped .d.ts delivery.

inputOutline.description: Plain-language outline of the tool's input fields.

localTools.description: Local tools the generated agent exposes to its own LLM loop. Empty for procedural agents.

frameworkConfig.description: For llm-loop agents: createAgent options to use (maxTurns, modifiers, processors). For procedural: 'none'.

systemPromptOutline.description: For llm-loop agents: outline of the generated agent's system prompt. For procedural: 'none'.

referenceExample.description: Example id from list_examples that the code phase should read and adapt.

testPlan.description: Validation steps, for example 'npm run typecheck passes'.

targetDirectory.description: Relative target directory inside the workspace. Defaults to agents/<agentId>.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Creates a new file or overwrites an existing file with the provided content.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to write (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported.

content.description: The content to write to the file.
